title: Reasons for finding — July 13, 2026
summary: The Canadian International Trade Tribunal ruled on July 13, 2026 that dumped or subsidized imports are injuring Canadian producers, likely triggering tariffs or import restrictions to protect domestic industries.

title: Reasons for determination — July 3, 2026
summary: Canada's import tribunal ruled that dumped steel fasteners from China harm domestic producers, triggering anti-dumping duties that will increase costs for Canadian manufacturers using these parts.

title: Finding — June 26, 2026
summary: Canadian importers of hot-rolled steel coils can now buy from more countries without facing anti-dumping duties, following the CITT's decision to end its investigation into unfairly priced imports.

title: Reasons for determination — June 24, 2026
summary: Canadian companies exporting steel products must now comply with stricter injury-assessment rules after the CITT found domestic steel producers faced material harm from imports.

title: Determination — June 9, 2026
summary: Canadian importers of forged steel pipe fittings now face new anti-dumping duties after the Canadian International Trade Tribunal found Chinese suppliers were selling below cost to unfairly undercut domestic producers.

title: Reasons for determination — June 1, 2026
summary: Canadian importers of certain steel products now face higher duties after the Canadian International Trade Tribunal found domestic producers were injured by unfairly priced imports.

title: Public hearing — May 25, 2026
summary: The Canadian International Trade Tribunal is holding a public hearing on May 25, 2026, where Canadian businesses can present evidence and arguments in a trade dispute case.
